# synix

> A build system for agent memory

## What it does

Synix transforms raw conversations into searchable, hierarchical memory with full provenance tracking. It processes conversation exports (ChatGPT, Claude, text files) through configurable LLM-backed transforms to create episode summaries, monthly rollups, and core memory artifacts. Change a prompt or add new conversations - only affected layers rebuild incrementally, like `make` or `dbt` but for AI agent memory.

## Key concepts

- **Pipeline** - Python-defined DAG of sources, transforms, and outputs
- **Layer** - Named processing stage (episodes, monthly rollups, core memory) 
- **Transform** - LLM-backed processing step (1:1, N:1, N:M patterns)
- **Artifact** - Immutable build output with content hash and provenance chain
- **SearchSurface** - Build-time searchable capability over artifact sets
- **Projection** - Materialized output (search index, context document)
- **Provenance** - Full chain from any artifact back to source conversations
- **Incremental rebuild** - Content-addressed caching skips unchanged work

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install with mesh support
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Add API key and sources, then build
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key
uvx synix build

# Search with provenance
uvx synix search "database migration"
uvx synix show final-report
uvx synix lineage artifact-id
```

## CLI commands

- `synix init <name>` - Create new pipeline with sources and config
- `synix build` - Run pipeline, rebuild only changed artifacts  
- `synix plan` - Show what would build without executing
- `synix search <query>` - Search artifacts with provenance chains
- `synix show <id>` - Display artifact content by label/ID prefix
- `synix lineage <id>` - Show full provenance tree for artifact
- `synix list [layer]` - List artifacts, optionally filtered by layer
- `synix verify` - Run validation checks on build outputs
- `synix clean` - Remove build directory
- `synix runs list` - Show immutable build snapshots
- `synix batch-build run` - Submit pipeline to OpenAI Batch API (50% cost)
- `synix mesh create` - Create distributed build mesh over Tailscale

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── core/models.py          # Pipeline, Layer, Transform, Artifact models
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py          # Execute pipeline, walk DAG, cache artifacts  
│   ├── artifacts.py       # Content-addressed artifact storage
│   ├── provenance.py      # Track lineage chains
│   └── llm_transforms.py  # Built-in episode/rollup/core transforms
├── ext/                   # Generic transform patterns (Map, Reduce, etc)
├── transforms/            # Platform transforms library
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py         # SQLite FTS5 + embedding search
│   └── retriever.py       # Hybrid keyword + semantic retrieval
├── adapters/              # ChatGPT/Claude/text parsers
├── cli/                   # Click commands with Rich formatting
└── templates/             # Demo pipelines for common patterns
```

## Pipeline definition

```python
# pipeline.py - code-first Python API
from synix import Pipeline, SearchSurface, Source, SynixSearch
from synix.ext import EpisodeSummary, MonthlyRollup, CoreSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("personal-memory")
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku"}

# Define processing layers
transcripts = Source("transcripts")  
episodes = EpisodeSummary("episodes", depends_on=[transcripts])
monthly = MonthlyRollup("monthly", depends_on=[episodes]) 
core = CoreSynthesis("core", depends_on=[monthly])

# Create searchable surface and output
search = SearchSurface("search", sources=[episodes, monthly, core])
pipeline.add(transcripts, episodes, monthly, core, search)
pipeline.add(SynixSearch("search", surface=search))
```

## Important constraints

- **SQLite only** - No external databases, filesystem-backed storage
- **Local execution** - No web UI, runs via CLI or Python API  
- **LLM required** - Transforms use Anthropic/OpenAI for processing
- **Source formats** - ChatGPT/Claude JSON exports, plain text/markdown
- **Python 3.11+** - Uses modern Python features and type hints
- **Content-addressed** - Artifacts identified by SHA-256 content hash
- **Incremental only** - No full rebuilds unless forced with --full flag
